G3825.full: again, back, once more, further, on the other hand.

G3844.full: gen: from; dat: beside, in the presence of; acc: alongside of.

G2281.full: (a) the sea, in contrast to the land, (b) a particular sea or lake, e.g. the sea of Galilee (Tiberias), the Red Sea.

G4863.full: I gather together, collect, assemble, receive with hospitality, entertain.

G846.brief: he, she, it, they, them, same
G846.full: he, she, it, they, them, same.

G3793.full: a crowd, mob, the common people.

G4118.full: the greatest, the most, very great.

G5620.full: so that, therefore, so then, so as to.

G1519.full: into, in, unto, to, upon, towards, for, among.

G1684.full: I step in; I go onboard a ship, embark.

G2521.full: I sit, am seated, enthroned; I dwell, reside.

G3956.brief: all, the whole, every kind of
G3956.full: all, the whole, every kind of.

G1909.brief: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
G1909.full: on, to, against, on the basis of, at.

G1093.full: the earth, soil, land, region, country, inhabitants of a region.

G3850.full: (a) a comparison, (b) a parable, often of those uttered by our Lord, (c) a proverb, an adage.

G3004.full: (denoting speech in progress), (a) I say, speak; I mean, mention, tell, (b) I call, name, especially in the pass., (c) I tell, command.

G1223.full: (a) gen: through, throughout, by the instrumentality of, (b) acc: through, on account of, by reason of, for the sake of, because of.

G305.full: I go up, mount, ascend; of things: I rise, spring up, come up.

G4846.full: I choke utterly, as weeds do plants; I crowd upon.

G2590.full: (a) fruit, generally vegetable, sometimes animal, (b) met: fruit, deed, action, result, (c) profit, gain.

G1325.full: I offer, give; I put, place.

G2570.full: beautiful, as an outward sign of the inward good, noble, honorable character; good, worthy, honorable, noble, and seen to be so.

G837.brief: I cause to increase, become greater, grow
G837.full: (a) I cause to increase, become greater (b) I increase, grow.

G5342.full: I carry, bear, bring; I conduct, lead; perhaps: I make publicly known.

G3775.full: (a) the ear, (b) met: the faculty of perception.

G2596.brief: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
G2596.full: gen: against, down from, throughout, by; acc: over against, among, daily, day-by-day, each day, according to, by way of.

G2065.full: (a) I ask (a question), question, (b) I request, make a request to, pray.

G1427.full: twelve; the usual way in which the Twelve apostles of Jesus are referred to.

G3466.full: a mystery, secret, of which initiation is necessary; in the NT: the counsels of God, once hidden but now revealed in the Gospel or some fact thereof; the Christian revelation generally; particular truths or details of the Christian revelation.

G932.full: kingship, sovereignty, authority, rule, especially of God, both in the world, and in the hearts of men; hence: kingdom, in the concrete sense.

G991.full: (primarily physical), I look, see, perceive, discern.

G3379.brief: lest at any time, lest, perhaps
G3379.full: lest at any time, lest; then weakened: whether perhaps, whether at all; in a principal clause: perhaps.

G1994.full: (a) trans: I turn (back) to (towards), (b) intrans: I turn (back) (to [towards]); I come to myself.

G863.full: (a) I send away, (b) I let go, release, permit to depart, (c) I remit, forgive, (d) I permit, suffer.

G4459.full: how, in what manner, by what means.

G1097.brief: I come to know, learn, realize
G1097.full: I am taking in knowledge, come to know, learn; aor: I ascertained, realized.

G3056.full: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy.

G142.full: I raise, lift up, take away, remove.

G5479.full: joy, gladness, a source of joy.

G2983.full: (a) I receive, get, (b) I take, lay hold of.

G1534.full: then, thereafter, next (marking a fresh stage); therefore, then, furthermore.

G4624.full: I cause to stumble, cause to sin, cause to become indignant, shock, offend.

G165.brief: an age, a cycle of time
G165.full: an age, a cycle (of time), especially of the present age as contrasted with the future age, and of one of a series of ages stretching to infinity.

G4149.full: riches, wealth, abundance, materially or spiritually.

G3062.full: left, left behind, the remainder, the rest, the others.

G1939.brief: desire, eagerness for, inordinate desire, lust
G1939.full: desire, eagerness for, inordinate desire, lust.

G1531.full: I journey in(to), I go in(to), enter, intervene.

G3385.full: if not, unless, whether at all.

G3426.full: a dry measure, nearly two English gallons.

G5087.full: I put, place, lay, set, fix, establish.

G3780.brief: by no means, not at all
G3780.full: by no means, not at all.

G2927.full: hidden, secret; as subst: the hidden (secret) things (parts), the inward nature (character).

G5319.brief: I make clear, visible, or manifest
G5319.full: I make clear (visible, manifest), make known.

G3761.full: neither, nor, not even, and not.

G5318.full: apparent, clear, visible, manifest; adv: clearly.

G5100.full: any one, some one, a certain one or thing.

G3358.full: a measure, whether lineal or cubic; a measuring rod.

G4369.brief: I put to, add, do again
G4369.full: I place (put) to, add; I do again.

G5613.brief: as, like as, how, while, so that
G5613.full: as, like as, about, as it were, according as, how, when, while, as soon as, so that.

G444.brief: a man, one of the human race
G444.full: a man, one of the human race.

G906.brief: I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop
G906.full: (a) I cast, throw, rush, (b) often, in the weaker sense: I place, put, drop.

G1453.full: (a) I wake, arouse, (b) I raise up.

G2250.full: a day, the period from sunrise to sunset.

G985.brief: I sprout, cause to sprout, make to grow up
G985.full: intrans: I sprout; trans: I cause to sprout, make to grow up.

G4134.full: full, abounding in, complete, completely occupied with.

G3860.full: I hand over, pledge, hand down, deliver, commit, commend, betray, abandon.

G649.brief: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss
G649.full: I send forth, send (as a messenger, commission, etc.), send away, dismiss.

G3754.full: that, since, because; may introduce direct discourse.

G3936.brief: I bring, present, come up to and stand by
G3936.full: I bring, present, prove, come up to and stand by, am present.

G2326.full: reaping, harvest; met: the harvest, crop.

G3666.full: I make like, liken; I compare.

G4615.full: mustard (probably the shrub, not the herb).

G4690.full: (a) seed, commonly of cereals, (b) offspring, descendents.

G3173.full: large, great, in the widest sense.

G4160.full: (a) I make, manufacture, construct, (b) I do, act, cause.

G2798.full: a young tender shoot, then: a branch; met: of descendants.

G1410.full: (a) I am powerful, have (the) power, (b) I am able, I can.

G4639.full: a shadow, shade, thick darkness, an outline.

G1911.full: (a) I throw upon, cast over, (b) I place upon, (c) I lay, (d) intrans: I strike upon, rush.

G3199.full: it is a care, it is an object of anxiety, it concerns.

G1326.full: I wake out of sleep, arouse in general, stir up.

bsb.1: Once again Jesus began to teach beside the sea, and such a large crowd gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat in it, while all the people crowded along the shore.
bsb.2: And He taught them many things in parables, and in His teaching He said,
bsb.3: “Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
bsb.4: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
bsb.5: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.
bsb.6: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
bsb.7: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings, and they yielded no crop.
bsb.8: Still other seed fell on good soil, where it sprouted, grew up, and produced a crop—one bearing thirtyfold, another sixtyfold, and another a hundredfold.”
bsb.9: Then Jesus said, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
bsb.10: As soon as Jesus was alone with the Twelve and those around Him, they asked Him about the parable.
bsb.11: He replied, “The mystery of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to those on the outside everything is expressed in parables,
bsb.12: so that, ‘they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven.’ ”
bsb.13: Then Jesus said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand any of the parables?
bsb.15: Some are like the seeds along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
bsb.16: Some are like the seeds sown on rocky ground. They hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
bsb.17: But they themselves have no root, and they remain for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
bsb.18: Others are like the seeds sown among the thorns. They hear the word,
bsb.19: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
bsb.20: Still others are like the seeds sown on good soil. They hear the word, receive it, and produce a crop—thirtyfold, sixtyfold, or a hundredfold.”
bsb.21: Jesus also said to them, “Does anyone bring in a lamp to put it under a basket or under a bed? Doesn’t he set it on a stand?
bsb.22: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be brought to light.
bsb.23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.”
bsb.24: He went on to say, “Pay attention to what you hear. With the measure you use, it will be measured to you, and even more will be added to you.
bsb.25: For whoever has will be given more. But whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.”
bsb.26: Jesus also said, “The kingdom of God is like a man who scatters seed on the ground.
bsb.27: Night and day he sleeps and wakes, and the seed sprouts and grows, though he knows not how.
bsb.28: All by itself the earth produces a crop—first the stalk, then the head, then grain that ripens within.
bsb.29: And as soon as the grain is ripe, he swings the sickle, because the harvest has come. ”
bsb.30: Then He asked, “To what can we compare the kingdom of God? With what parable shall we present it?
bsb.31: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest of all seeds sown upon the earth.
bsb.32: But after it is planted, it grows to be the largest of all garden plants and puts forth great branches, so that the birds of the air nest in its shade.”
bsb.33: With many such parables Jesus spoke the word to them, to the extent that they could understand.
bsb.34: He did not tell them anything without using a parable. But privately He explained everything to His own disciples.
bsb.35: When that evening came, He said to His disciples, “Let us cross to the other side.”
bsb.36: After they had dismissed the crowd, they took Jesus with them, since He was already in the boat. And there were other boats with Him.
bsb.37: Soon a violent windstorm came up, and the waves were breaking over the boat, so that it was being swamped.
bsb.38: But Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on the cushion. So they woke Him and said, “Teacher, don’t You care that we are perishing?”
bsb.39: Then Jesus got up and rebuked the wind and the sea. “Silence!” He commanded. “Be still!” And the wind died down, and it was perfectly calm.
bsb.40: “Why are you so afraid?” He asked. “Do you still have no faith?”
bsb.41: Overwhelmed with fear, they asked one another, “Who is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?”

web.1: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.
web.2: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,
web.3: “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,
web.4: and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and devoured it.
web.5: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.
web.6: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
web.7: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
web.8: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”
web.9: He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”
web.10: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
web.11: He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,
web.12: that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’”
web.13: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?
web.15: The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
web.16: These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.
web.17: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.
web.18: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,
web.19: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
web.20: Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”
web.21: He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?
web.22: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.
web.23: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”
web.24: He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.
web.25: For whoever has, to him will more be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”
web.26: He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,
web.27: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how.
web.28: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
web.29: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
web.30: He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?
web.31: It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,
web.32: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”
web.33: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.
web.34: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.
web.35: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”
web.36: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.
web.37: A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.
web.38: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”
web.39: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
web.40: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”
web.41: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

asv.1: And again he began to teach by the sea side. And there is gathered unto him a very great multitude, so that he entered into a boat, and sat in the sea; and all the multitude were by the sea on the land.
asv.2: And he taught them many things in parables, and said unto them in his teaching,
asv.3: Hearken: Behold, the sower went forth to sow:
asv.4: and it came to pass, as he sowed, some [seed] fell by the way side, and the birds came and devoured it.
asv.5: And other fell on the rocky [ground], where it had not much earth; and straightway it sprang up, because it had no deepness of earth:
asv.6: and when the sun was risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
asv.7: And other fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
asv.8: And others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing; and brought forth, thirtyfold, and sixtyfold, and a hundredfold.
asv.9: And he said, Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
asv.10: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parables.
asv.11: And he said unto them, Unto you is given the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all things are done in parables:
asv.12: that seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest haply they should turn again, and it should be forgiven them.
asv.13: And he saith unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how shall ye know all the parables?
asv.15: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; and when they have heard, straightway cometh Satan, and taketh away the word which hath been sown in them.
asv.16: And these in like manner are they that are sown upon the rocky [places], who, when they have heard the word, straightway receive it with joy;
asv.17: and they have no root in themselves, but endure for a while; then, when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, straightway they stumble.
asv.18: And others are they that are sown among the thorns; these are they that have heard the word,
asv.19: and the cares of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
asv.20: And those are they that were sown upon the good ground; such as hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, thirtyfold, and sixtyfold, and a hundredfold.
asv.21: And he said unto them, Is the lamp brought to be put under the bushel, or under the bed, [and] not to be put on the stand?
asv.22: For there is nothing hid, save that it should be manifested; neither was [anything] made secret, but that it should come to light.
asv.23: If any man hath ears to hear, let him hear.
asv.24: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete it shall be measured unto you; and more shall be given unto you.
asv.25: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken away even that which he hath.
asv.26: And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed upon the earth;
asv.27: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, he knoweth not how.
asv.28: The earth beareth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
asv.29: But when the fruit is ripe, straightway he putteth forth the sickle, because the harvest is come.
asv.30: And he said, How shall we liken the kingdom of God? or in what parable shall we set it forth?
asv.31: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown upon the earth, though it be less than all the seeds that are upon the earth,
asv.32: yet when it is sown, groweth up, and becometh greater than all the herbs, and putteth out great branches; so that the birds of the heaven can lodge under the shadow thereof.
asv.33: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it;
asv.34: and without a parable spake he not unto them: but privately to his own disciples he expounded all things.
asv.35: And on that day, when even was come, he saith unto them, Let us go over unto the other side.
asv.36: And leaving the multitude, they take him with them, even as he was, in the boat. And other boats were with him.
asv.37: And there ariseth a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the boat, insomuch that the boat was now filling.
asv.38: And he himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion: and they awake him, and say unto him, Teacher, carest thou not that we perish?
asv.39: And he awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
asv.40: And he said unto them, Why are ye fearful? have ye not yet faith?
asv.41: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

kjv.1: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
kjv.2: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
kjv.3: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
kjv.4: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
kjv.5: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
kjv.6: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
kjv.7: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
kjv.8: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
kjv.9: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
kjv.10: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
kjv.11: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
kjv.12: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.
kjv.13: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
kjv.15: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
kjv.16: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
kjv.17: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word’s sake, immediately they are offended.
kjv.18: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
kjv.19: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
kjv.20: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
kjv.21: And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
kjv.22: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
kjv.23: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
kjv.24: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.
kjv.25: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
kjv.26: And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
kjv.27: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.
kjv.28: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
kjv.29: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.
kjv.30: And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
kjv.31: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
kjv.32: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
kjv.33: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it.
kjv.34: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
kjv.35: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
kjv.36: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
kjv.37: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
kjv.38: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
kjv.39: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
kjv.40: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?
kjv.41: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

notes.title: The hardest ἵνα in Mark — purpose, result, or citation formula?
notes.body:
Jesus explains the parables: to those outside everything happens in parables, **ἵνα** "seeing they may see and not perceive… *lest* they turn and be forgiven" (quoting Isaiah 6:9–10).

Taken as a full **purpose** clause ("*in order that* they may not perceive"), Jesus speaks in parables *to prevent* understanding — theologically hard. The main alternatives:

- **Purpose** (face value): parables conceal as judgment — matches Isaiah 6's commission of judicial hardening, and Mark's insider/outsider theme (4:11).
- **Result**: "*with the result that* they see but don't perceive" — grammatically possible in Koine, softer.
- **Citation shorthand**: ἵνα introduces the Isaiah quote almost like "so that [the scripture is fulfilled which says]…" — compare Matthew 13:13, which changes it to ὅτι ("because they do not see"), visibly softening Mark.

Note μήποτε at the end: "lest" (purpose-negative) or "unless perhaps" (leaving a door open — some rabbinic readings of Isaiah took it that way). Every English version of this verse is a theology-laden decision; there is no neutral rendering.

notes.title: πεφίμωσο — a perfect imperative
notes.body:
Jesus rebukes the sea: **σιώπα, πεφίμωσο** — "Be silent! Be muzzled!" The second verb is a **perfect passive imperative**, one of the rarest forms in the NT (the perfect imperative occurs only here and Acts 15:29).

The perfect's stative force makes the command something like *"be muzzled — and stay that way"*: a state to be entered and maintained. English can only gesture at it ("Be still!" in most versions flattens both the muzzling metaphor and the aspect). A study translation might risk *"Silence! Stay muzzled!"*

φιμόω is also the word used for muzzling an ox (1 Corinthians 9:9) and for Jesus "silencing" demons (Mark 1:25 — same verb, aorist there). The echo suggests Mark presents the storm-calming like an exorcism.
